act_name: Protection of Children from Sexual Offences Act 2012 (POCSO)
section: Section 2 — Definition of Child and Sexual Offences
text: POCSO Act protects children below 18 years from sexual abuse and exploitation. Sexual offences under POCSO include: penetrative sexual assault (Section 3), aggravated penetrative sexual assault (Section 5) — punishment up to life imprisonment, sexual assault (Section 7), aggravated sexual assault (Section 9), sexual harassment (Section 11), use of child for pornographic purposes (Section 13). The Act provides for child-friendly procedures during investigation and trial. The identity of the child victim must not be disclosed to the public.

act_name: Protection of Children from Sexual Offences Act 2012 (POCSO)
text: Any person who has apprehension that an offence under POCSO is likely to be committed or has knowledge that such an offence has been committed shall provide such information to the Special Juvenile Police Unit (SJPU) or the local police. Failure to report is an offence punishable with 6 months imprisonment. Childline helpline number: 1098 (24x7, free). Any person reporting in good faith is protected from legal action. Medical examination of child victim must be conducted without delay and with consent of parent/guardian.

act_name: Juvenile Justice (Care and Protection of Children) Act 2015
text: The JJ Act deals with two categories: (1) Child in conflict with law — a child below 18 years alleged to have committed an offence; (2) Child in need of care and protection — children who are abandoned, orphaned, victims of abuse, trafficking, or at risk. Children between 16-18 years alleged to have committed heinous offences may be tried as adults by the Children's Court after assessment by the Juvenile Justice Board. A child cannot be sentenced to death or life imprisonment.

section: Section 3 — Prohibition of employment of children in certain occupations
text: No child who has not completed his fourteenth year shall be employed or permitted to work in any of the occupations set forth in Part A of the Schedule or in any workshop wherein any of the processes set forth in Part B of the Schedule is carried on. This includes factories, mines, hazardous work. The Child Labour (Prohibition and Regulation) Amendment Act 2016 extended the ban to all work by children below 14 years. Children between 14-18 years cannot work in hazardous occupations. Penalty for violation: imprisonment 6 months to 2 years and fine.

section: Section 3 — Penalty for giving or taking dowry
text: If any person, after the commencement of this Act, gives or takes or abets the giving or taking of dowry, he shall be punishable with imprisonment for a term which shall not be less than five years, and with fine which shall not be less than fifteen thousand rupees or the amount of the value of such dowry, whichever is more. Dowry means any property or valuable security given or agreed to be given either directly or indirectly in connection with the marriage. Demanding dowry is a criminal offence. File FIR under Section 3 of Dowry Prohibition Act AND under BNS Section 85 (cruelty) if harassment occurs.
